title: Randia turgida
intro: Randia turgida, commonly known as the "Milk Tree," is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Randia turgida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Randia turgida is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and other ailments.
taxonomy: Randia turgida is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is part of the genus Randia, which consists of various species found in tropical and subtropical regions. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the madder or coffee family. It is native to Central America and the Caribbean, where it can be found growing in a variety of habitats. The classification of Randia turgida reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Rubiaceae family.
